name: Antigua and Barbuda
avoid: June–November overlaps the wetter hurricane-risk season, so ferries to Barbuda, stingray trips, and exposed beach days are more likely to be disrupted by wind or rough seas.
Antigua Carnival from late July into early August pushes room rates up in St. John's and makes downtown, beaches, and taxi availability noticeably busier.

Local buses are cheap but mostly daylight-only and do not reliably reach remote beaches or late-night dinner spots, so avoid planning English Harbour or Barbuda transfers too tightly without a taxi or rental car backup.
stopoverNote: For trips under 5 days, stay on Antigua, use St. John's or Dickenson Bay as the main base, and treat Barbuda as a weather-dependent day trip instead of changing hotels twice.

cities.name: St. John's
cities.notes: The capital is the practical base for museum stops, market walks, Fort James sunsets, taxi logistics, and onward transport to English Harbour or the Barbuda Express terminal.

cities.name: English Harbour
cities.notes: Best base for Nelson's Dockyard, Shirley Heights, Pigeon Beach, yacht-marina dining, and Antigua's most rewarding concentration of historic naval sights.

cities.name: Barbuda (Codrington)
cities.notes: Codrington is the jumping-off point for the Frigate Bird Sanctuary, pink-sand beaches, and the laid-back Barbuda pace that feels very different from Antigua.

itinerary.sim: Digicel Antigua or FLOW prepaid data are the easiest options on arrival; budget roughly ₹1,200–₹2,500 for maps, WhatsApp, and ferry confirmations, and download offline maps before heading to Barbuda.

St. John's.name: St. John's
St. John's.note: Use St. John's as the arrival-and-logistics base because taxis, bus stations, supermarkets, and the Barbuda Express dock are all easiest from here.

days.theme: Heritage Quay, cathedral views and a classic west-coast sunset

activities.name: Museum of Antigua and Barbuda
activities.tip: A roughly ₹310 museum ticket is typical; pair it with the market and cathedral while downtown is cooler in the morning.

activities.name: St. John's Cathedral and Public Market walk

days.theme: Betty's Hope, Stingray City and an easy island-road day

activities.name: Betty's Hope Historic Sugar Plantation
activities.tip: The usual donation/entry works out to roughly ₹155, so carry small cash because the site often operates on an honesty-box style system.

activities.name: Stingray City Antigua snorkel trip
activities.tip: The standard adult rate works out to about ₹5,860 without transfers, so pre-book if you want a morning slot in the December–April high season.

activities.name: Taxi return between St. John's and stingray departure area

English Harbour.name: English Harbour
English Harbour.note: Switch here for Antigua's best historical cluster and marina atmosphere; this is where a rental car becomes more useful than relying on buses.

days.theme: Nelson's Dockyard, Dow's Hill and Shirley Heights

activities.name: Nelson's Dockyard National Park pass
activities.tip: Tourist admission works out to about ₹1,240 and covers the Dockyard area plus nearby national-park sights such as Shirley Heights and Dow's Hill on the same day.

activities.name: Admiral's House Museum and Dockyard quayside walk

days.theme: Pigeon Beach, Galleon Beach and a slower harbor day

activities.name: Rental car day for English Harbour and east-coast detours

Barbuda (Codrington).name: Barbuda (Codrington)
Barbuda (Codrington).note: Treat Barbuda as a weather-sensitive extension: catch the earliest workable ferry, keep luggage light, and expect boat trips to dictate the day's rhythm.

days.theme: Codrington Lagoon birds, pink sand and a classic Barbuda beach day

activities.name: Frigate Bird Sanctuary boat tour from Codrington Lagoon
activities.tip: Most visitors go by licensed boat; many tours land around ₹3,255–₹4,650 pp, and some operators also collect a separate ₹465 council fee at the fisheries dock.

activities.name: Princess Diana Beach / Pink Sand Beach stop
Martello Tower exterior and village walk

connections.method: Local bus from St. John's bus station toward Falmouth/English Harbour, fixed-fare taxi, or rental car via Sir Sydney Walling Highway (about 45–60m)
connections.cost: ₹93–₹155 pp by bus; ₹2,100–₹2,500 per taxi; ₹3,350–₹4,200/day for a rental car

connections.method: Taxi or self-drive back to St. John's ferry terminal, then Barbuda Express ferry to Barbuda (about 90m sailing, weather permitting)
connections.cost: ₹2,100–₹2,500 per taxi to terminal + ₹5,115 pp one way / ₹9,300 pp round-trip ferry

extras: Antigua taxis run on government-set route pricing rather than meters, so confirm the fixed fare before you leave; many published rates are per car for up to about four passengers.
Local buses are really privately run minibuses charging about ₹93–₹155 per ride; they are cheapest in daylight but are not reliable for late dinners or early-morning airport or ferry departures.
Rental cars usually need a temporary local permit that agencies arrange for about ₹1,675, and Antigua drives on the left.
Barbuda Express fares effective from December 2025 are about ₹5,115 one way and ₹9,300 round trip for adults, so book ferry seats ahead in the December–April dry season.
Frigate Bird Sanctuary visits are best from November to April, and December–April is also the driest, easiest weather window for combining Antigua beaches with a Barbuda side trip.
